messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Sometimes get worktree failure error for folder session ISSUE BODY: My CLI AHP sessions have been kicking off successfully today, but I got one error while kicking off a new one: `Error: (sendFailed) Error: git worktree exited with code 128: fatal: Could not reset index file to revision 'HEAD'.` Interestingly, this happened for a folder (non-worktree) session. @karthiknadig is getting this repeatedly too (I think he got this for every session he tried to kick off, whereas I only got it 1 in maybe 10 sessions). --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### Option C: Download Per-Job Logs via API (works while run is in progress) When the run is still in progress but the failed job has completed, use the GitHub API to download that job's step logs directly: ```bash # Save the full job log to a temp file (can be very large — 30k+ lines) gh api repos/microsoft/vscode/actions/jobs//logs > "$TMPDIR/ci-job-log.txt" ``` Then search the saved file. **Start with `##[error]`** — this is the GitHub Actions error annotation that marks the exact line where the step failed: ```bash # Step 1: Find the error annotation (fastest path to the failure) grep -n '##\[error\]' "$TMPDIR/ci-job-log.txt" # Step 2: Read context around the error (e.g., if error is on line 34371, read 200 lines before it) sed -n '34171,34371p' "$TMPDIR/ci-job-log.txt" ``` If `##[error]` doesn't reveal enough, use broader patterns: ```bash # Find test failures, exceptions, and crash indicators grep -n -E 'HTTPError|ECONNRESET|ETIMEDOUT|502|exit code|Process completed|node:internal|triggerUncaughtException' "$TMPDIR/ci-job-log.txt" | head -20 ``` > **Why save to a file?** The API response for a full job log can be 30k+ lines. Tool output gets truncated, so always redirect to a file first, then search. --- File: openproject_full.md --- ### 4.3 File Services #### 4.3.1 GET Files (Header) Service _Not implemented_ --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### Option A: View Failed Step Logs Directly Best for build/compile/lint failures where the error is in the step output: ```bash # View only the failed step logs (most useful — shows just the errors) gh run view --job --log-failed ``` > **Important**: `--log-failed` requires the **entire run** to complete, not just the failed job. If other jobs are still running, this command will block or error. Use **Option C** below to get logs for a completed job while the run is still in progress. The output can be large. Pipe through `tail` or `grep` to focus: ```bash # Last 100 lines of failed output gh run view --job --log-failed | tail -100 # Search for common error patterns gh run view --job --log-failed | grep -E "Error|FAIL|error TS|AssertionError|failing" ``` --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- #### Expected Results - Active root is the worktree path, not the repository path - File is created under the worktree's `.github/instructions/` folder (not the bare repo) - File auto-saves and auto-commits to the worktree - Item count updates in both the sidebar badge and editor list after creation - The new file appears in the list with a friendly name derived from the filename --- File: openproject_full.md --- ### Error codes specific to Nextcloud The following table lists the error codes of failures specific to Nextcloud, with a description of the possible reasons and suggested solutions. | Error code | Error description | Possible reasons | Next steps and solutions | |------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | ERR_NC_HOST_NOT_FOUND | No Nextcloud server was found at the configured host URL. | There might be a typo or the URL has changed. | Check the configuration and enter a valid URL. | | ERR_NC_DEPENDENCY_MISSING | A required dependency is missing on the file storage. | Either the Integration OpenProject app or the Team Folders app is not enabled in Nextcloud. | Add the missing dependency to the Nextcloud server. | | ERR_NC_TEAM_FOLDER_NOT_FOUND | The team folder could not be found. | The team folder used by the Integration OpenProject app was not found. This folder is usually named `OpenProject` and is owned by the group `OpenProject`. | The team folder is used only by the automatically managed project folders mode. Try to disable this option in the Nextcloud administration for the Integration OpenProject app and reenable it afterwards. |

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Fix intermittent git worktree reset failure for folder sessions",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "git-integration", "session-management", "bug"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a VS Code AI user, I want folder sessions to reliably initialize without git worktree reset errors so that my AHP (AI Helper Protocol) sessions can start consistently regardless of repository state.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a folder session is being initialized When the repository has any valid Git state Then the session starts successfully without 'git worktree exited with code 128' error",
    "Given a folder session fails to initialize due to git worktree reset error When retrying after a short delay (e.g., 5 seconds) Then the session initializes successfully on subsequent attempts",
    "Given a repository is in an inconsistent state (not at HEAD, corrupted index) When initializing a folder session Then the system detects and handles the inconsistency gracefully without crashing",
    "Given multiple concurrent folder sessions are being created When they all attempt to initialize simultaneously Then each session completes without worktree reset failures"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate why folder sessions trigger git worktree operations instead of regular file system operations",
      "Add retry logic with exponential backoff for git worktree reset operations (max 3 retries)",
      "Implement repository state validation before attempting worktree reset",
      "Add fallback mechanism: if worktree reset fails, attempt direct index manipulation or alternative initialization path",
      "Log detailed diagnostics when worktree reset fails including current HEAD commit, index status, and working directory state"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Display user-friendly error message explaining the session initialization issue",
      "Show retry option for failed sessions with clear instructions",
      "Add visual feedback during retry attempts (loading/spinning indicator)"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Create test case: Initialize folder session on clean repository - verify success",
      "Create test case: Initialize folder session on repository at HEAD - verify success",
      "Create test case: Initialize folder session on repository not at HEAD - verify graceful handling",
      "Create test case: Concurrent folder session initialization - verify no race condition failures",
      "Create test case: Repository with corrupted index - verify retry mechanism works"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed by team lead",
    "All unit tests passing (including edge cases)",
    "Integration tests covering concurrent session scenarios",
    "E2E tests verifying folder session initialization in various repository states",
    "Documentation updated with troubleshooting steps for this error"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Why does a 'folder (non-worktree) session' trigger git worktree operations? Is there a bug in the session type detection?",
    "What is the expected behavior when a folder session needs to create/update files - should it use regular file system operations or worktree?",
    "Has this issue been observed with different repository types (GitHub, GitLab, local repos) and different OpenProject integrations?",
    "Are there any known patterns that trigger this error more frequently (specific repo states, recent commits, etc.)?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The folder session should use regular file system operations for creating/updating files",
    "Git worktree operations are only needed when explicitly requested or as part of specific feature flows",
    "The retry mechanism with backoff will be sufficient to handle transient failures"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Phase 1: Investigation and Root Cause Analysis",
      "description": "Determine why folder sessions incorrectly trigger worktree operations and identify the code path causing this behavior"
    },
    {
      "name": "Phase 2: Implement Retry Logic",
      "description": "Add retry mechanism with exponential backoff for git worktree reset failures"
    },
    {
      "name": "Phase 3: Add Fallback Path",
      "description": "Implement alternative initialization path when worktree operations fail"
    },
    {
      "name": "Phase 4: Testing and Validation",
      "description": "Comprehensive testing across different repository states and concurrent scenarios"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [
    {"task_group": "be", "estimated_hours": 12},
    {"task_group": "fe", "estimated_hours": 4},
    {"task_group": "qa", "estimated_hours": 8}
  ],
  "context_sources": ["vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "openproject_full.md"]
}
```
